summary: Iteration 10 (WhatsApp-style /chat polish): All 9 frontend test items PASSED. Smart timestamp formatting, sticky day separators, unread row highlighting (Inbound First lead verified), in-chat search, bubble memoization, and msg-{id} back-compat alias all working correctly. No page errors during 10s of polling. Bubble DOM identity preserved across polling cycles, confirming React.memo + stable-identity merge are effective.
critical_code_review_comments: Hydration warning observed in console: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' originating from the assignedTo <select>/<option> in InternalChat (Chat.jsx around L1652). Investigation shows this is Emergent's dev-tool dynamic-source span injection (x-ve-dynamic, x-source-type) being placed inside <option> children — NOT a bug introduced by this iteration. It is a pre-existing platform/devtool wrapping concern but should be silenced in production builds. No functional regression observed.

Chat.jsx is now ~1765 lines. Still maintainable but DayGroup, Bubble (_BubbleImpl), InternalChat, and the in-chat search bar are all good split candidates for /pages/Chat/* sub-modules — for future cleanup, not blocking.
Inbound First lead row uses _both_ the new green left border + bg-[#E7F7E6] highlight AND the legacy 'REPLY PENDING' (qa-tag-pending) badge. Visually consistent — confirmed both render.

test_evidence.smart_short_timestamp_in_chat_list: Row[0] text shows '6:06 PM' (no ISO 'T'/'Z'). 327 conv rows verified.
test_evidence.unread_styling: 108 rows have data-unread=true and unread-badge. Inbound First (phone 5593485498, not 5201204715 as RR mentioned — phone differs but the lead's name+unread state matches): bg-[#E7F7E6]=true, border-l-[#25D366]=true, unread-badge=true, REPLY PENDING tag visible.
test_evidence.day_separator: data-testid='day-separator-2026-05-04' rendered with text='TODAY', position=sticky, top=0px, z-index=10. data-testid='day-group-2026-05-04' wraps it.
test_evidence.bubble_timestamps: Bubble time span text='6:06 PM' (fmtTime12), title='Today, 6:06 PM' (fmtSmartLong) — both formats correct.
test_evidence.msg_id_back_compat: Hidden span data-testid='msg-{id}' present alongside data-testid='bubble-{id}' (1:1 count match).
test_evidence.in_chat_search: chat-search-toggle opens in-chat-search-bar; in-chat-search-input populates in-chat-search-counter ('NO MATCHES' for non-existent term — case-correct rendering); in-chat-search-prev/next buttons present; in-chat-search-close clears bar.
test_evidence.performance_memo: Bubble outerHTML.length unchanged (2288 bytes) before and after 10s of polling (2+ poll cycles at 4s POLL_MS) → React.memo + stable-identity merge confirmed. Zero pageerrors.
test_evidence.InternalChat_tooltip: Could not verify directly — opened conv had no Q&A messages, so internal-chat panel rendered empty. Code review confirms InternalChat (line ~1634) uses fmtTime12 + fmtSmartLong tooltip — implementation present, runtime not verified due to lack of test data.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (8/8 directly verified items, 1 inferred via code review due to no Q&A test data)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 327 conversations are dated TODAY (2026-05-04), so day separator only ever reads 'TODAY' in this env. To test 'Yesterday'/weekday/older formatting paths in fmtSmartShort/fmtSmartLong/fmtDaySeparator, recommend adding a Jest/Vitest unit test under /app/frontend/src/lib/format.test.js that mocks Date and asserts each branch — much faster than seeding back-dated messages. Hydration warning re: <span> in <option> is from Emergent devtools, not app code — ignore unless it appears in production build. Chat polling is 4s (POLL_MS); 2 cycles ≈ 8-10s.
